tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent re-spikes to ~$102 (est.) on Trump's naval blockade of Hormuz (Apr 13), reversing the ceasefire crash. Vance-Iran talks failed after 21 hours; Iran refuses to open the strait. Reinstated long from ceasefire dip toward $95. PTTEP and Medco re-benefit as oil climbs; Sapura options overlay re-activates. Tail scenario $140-185/bbl now probability-weighted at 48%. Netanyahu-Lebanon talks (Apr 14) are the key derisking catalyst - a deal removes Iran's core pretext and could rapidly compress oil. Hold core long; add options for tail. Conviction high.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Blockade re-escalation on Apr 13 validates maintaining ASEAN Energy overweight vs. banks. Brent +7% to ~$102, ASEAN Energy Equities +8.5%; iTraxx Asia IG widens to ~195bp, ASEAN Banks -3.6%. Reinstate full ASEAN Energy long vs. bank underweight. iTraxx at 195bp is back above the 195bp re-add threshold - hold protection overlay. Ceasefire collapse probability (tail) at 48%. Netanyahu-Lebanon talks (Apr 14) are the clearest derisking signal to trim energy and reduce the bank underweight.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short - Dip Opportunity on Trump-Driven Rally
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP weakens to ~58.50 (est.) on blockade escalation (Apr 13), partially reinstating short PHP thesis. Brent ~$102 and JKM ~$20.50 rebuild the Philippine import bill. Reinstate half-size short PHP position via NDF; options 61.00 strike re-activates at 48% tail probability. Philippines bilateral Hormuz deal provides some protection but effective transit is near zero. Derisking trigger: Netanyahu-Lebanon deal (Apr 14) or confirmed formal Hormuz reopening.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short - Re-Entry on Trump Pause Dip
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR weakens to ~17,000 (est.) and Indonesia 10Y rises to ~7.35% (est.) as blockade escalation reverses ceasefire relief (Apr 13). Brent ~$102 reopens the ICP gap to ~$32/bbl - fiscal stress rebuilds. Reinstate short IDR; stop-loss on Brent re-exit was $105 - now broken. Options: USD Call/IDR Put 17,500 strike re-activates as 48% tail probability materialises. Full short IDR reinstatement; monitor Netanyahu-Lebanon talks (Apr 14) as derisking signal. Conviction high.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection - Hold After Deadline
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG widens to ~195bp (+17bp) and ASEAN HY to ~590bp (+45bp) on blockade escalation (Apr 13). Re-add to the 195bp trigger level; full protection position reinstated. A US-Iran naval confrontation would push iTraxx above 220bp. Netanyahu-Lebanon talks (Apr 14) are the primary derisking catalyst; a confirmed deal could tighten spreads 20-30bp rapidly. Maintain core short in Sapura Energy / ASEAN HY paper at 48% tail probability.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold Into Phase Two
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM re-spikes to ~$20.50/MMBtu (est.) and TTF to ~€47.50/MWh on blockade escalation (Apr 13). Reinstating full JKM long vs. TTF short; the blockade re-establishes Asia scarcity premium. Ras Laffan force majeure remains in effect; no restart confirmation. Tail scenario target of $24-28/MMBtu JKM back in play at 48% probability. Netanyahu-Lebanon talks (Apr 14) are the primary derisking signal - a deal would allow Ras Laffan restart talks to resume. Stop on confirmed formal Hormuz reopening.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: Blockade re-escalation has closed the window: iTraxx Asia IG has re-widened to ~195bp and ASEAN HY to ~590bp. The Apr 12-13 no-deal outcome and naval blockade order have raised tail risk to 48%. Do not execute refinancing now; wait for Netanyahu-Lebanon deal (Apr 14) or confirmed Hormuz reopening as a potential second window. Monitor spreads - window reopens below 170bp.

PTTEP: ceasefire improves the THB window - TH 10Y eased to ~2.22% and USD/THB strengthened to ~33.20 (est.). Brent at ~$97 reduces upstream EBITDA vs. peak but remains materially above the pre-shock $65 level. Execute H2 2026 USD prefunding now; the 2-week ceasefire window is real but fragile. Gulf JV sanctions-compliance review remains urgent given uncertain Hormuz normalization.

Ceasefire creates a narrow execution window: iTraxx Asia IG has narrowed to ~178bp and USD/SGD has strengthened to ~1.2780 (est.). Brent at ~$97 and JKM at ~$19.50 reduce working capital pressure vs. peak. Execute revolving-credit expansion now while the window is open - it is conditional on the ceasefire holding through Apr 12 Vance talks. Maintain USD hedging; ceasefire collapse risk is 30%.

Ceasefire relief: ASEAN HY has narrowed to ~545bp and Brent to ~$97. However, Hyflux's utility stress is structural - Kuwait desalination precedent is still live, Hormuz is claimed closed by Iran, and ceasefire is only 2-week conditional. Use the spread-tightening window to execute private-credit and quasi-sovereign restructuring now; do not treat the ceasefire as full resolution. Collapse risk 30% within 2 weeks.
